children.value: > **How to use:** Mark `Status` with ✅ Covered / 🔄 In Progress / ⬜ Not Yet / ⏭️ Skipped.
The main material for this class is the textbook
Getting Started with SQL and Databases
by Mark Simon, which is available for free using the
. Each week, we will cover specific chapters and sections from the book, along with additional resources and exercises.
First contact: reading data, selecting columns, filtering rows, ordering results.
Understanding how data is organized before writing serious queries.
Deep dive into WHERE: NULLs, data types in filters, logical operators, wildcards.
WHERE — NULL handling (IS NULL / IS NOT NULL)
Sorting deeply, limiting results, and computing new values inside queries.
Conceptual peak: combining multiple tables with all join types.
Counting, summarizing, grouping, and filtering grouped results.
Week 6 — Working with Tables & Set Operations

children.label: Week 6 — Working with Tables & Set Operations (Ch. 8 & 9)

children.value: DDL, DML, access control, and advanced set operations.
Targets — CONSTRAINTS (NOT NULL, UNIQUE, DEFAULT, CHECK)
